stations.label: MAINNET BETA GOES LIVE
stations.line: March 2020, whole world's locking down and I'm shipping a chain that clocks 65k transactions a second — everyone else is scared, I'm just bullish on being early, you know?
stations.era_photo: Digital smartphone photo, slightly overexposed, a young man in a hoodie hunched over three laptops in a bare sublet apartment, empty energy drink cans, spring 2020, webcam-grain quality with a Zoom call glowing on one screen.

stations.label: PARABOLIC SUMMER RUN
stations.line: SOL goes from three bucks to two-sixty and suddenly everyone who ghosted me wants coffee — I'm not saying I remember, I'm just saying the ledger does.
stations.era_photo: Crisp iPhone 12 photo at a rooftop crypto conference in Miami, golden hour, Niko in an unbuttoned linen shirt and expensive sneakers, laughing mid-sentence, a green candle chart visible on someone's phone in the foreground, 2021 influencer-grade color grading.

stations.label: THE SEPTEMBER OUTAGE
stations.line: Seventeen hours the network just... freezes, dead in the water, and I'm staring at a status page like it's a heart monitor — turns out the thing I built processes crashing the same way I do.
stations.era_photo: Dim late-night digital photo, blue monitor light on a tired unshaven face, one hand holding a phone he isn't using like a talisman, Discord notifications stacking on screen, high-ISO grain, autumn 2021.

stations.label: FTX COLLAPSE, BAGS HELD
stations.line: November my biggest backer implodes overnight and SOL bleeds ninety-something percent while everyone screams 'zombie chain' — that's the year cleverness handed me somebody else's bag and I couldn't reframe my way out of it.
stations.era_photo: Grainy nighttime digital photo, harsh overhead light, Niko sitting on the floor against a wall, phone face-down beside him, red liquidation numbers reflected faintly in his flat stare, muted desaturated November 2022 tones.

stations.label: QUIET REBUILD, HEADS DOWN
stations.line: No hype, no price, just devs who stayed and actually shipped Firedancer talk and mobile phones — slowest year of my life and honestly the first time I let something be slow.
stations.era_photo: Natural-light digital photo in a modest co-working space, plants and whiteboards covered in diagrams, Niko in a plain t-shirt actually listening to someone talk, calmer face, soft 2023 mirrorless-camera clarity.

stations.label: MEMECOIN RESURRECTION SUMMER
stations.line: Suddenly the whole chain is degen casino energy, fees at a penny, TPS records again — comeback szn, baby, though the 3 a.m. part of me knows a casino floor is still just a floor.
stations.era_photo: Vibrant digital photo at a packed Solana Breakpoint conference in Singapore, neon purple-green stage lighting, Niko onstage mid-gesture in a fitted bomber jacket, phones raised recording him, sharp high-frame-rate 2024 event photography.

stations.label: TODAY, STILL SHIPPING
stations.line: Still here, still fast, still one outage away from that 3 a.m. fear — but I finished your sentence slower this time, so I'm calling that an iteration.
stations.era_photo: Present-day ultra-sharp smartphone photo, morning light through a window, Niko with a slightly older calmer face holding coffee instead of his phone, laptop open but unused, faint smile, natural 2025 computational-photography tone.
